name: Haggis
recipeIngredient: 1 Plastic baking bag, OR 1 sheep's stomach, thoroughly cleaned
Broth in which liver and heart were cooked (up to 1 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
This recipe serves 6-10. If a sheep's stomach is available, double the amounts.
Increase both pepper and onion as preferred (most haggis includes more of these).
If butcher did not trim heart, liver, and lungs, do so first by cutting open the heart to rinse it for quicker cooking.
Place liver, heart, and lungs in a large pot with 1-1/2 cups water. Bring to boil and simmer for about 1 hour and 30 minutes. Let cool and reserve broth.
Grind liver and heart through a meat grinder. Grind lungs (lights) similarly. Also grind beef suet.
Peel, slice, and chop onions; add them to the pot with the ground meats. Add salt and spices, mixing thoroughly.
Toast or lightly brown oatmeal in a heavy-bottomed pan. Mix into the meat mixture until well combined.
Add reserved broth as needed so that the mixture holds together when a handful is squeezed.
Stuff into plastic baking bag (or sheep stomach) leaving about 1/4 full; seal with smooth side out and close the opening. Wrap loosely in cheesecloth, pricking with a skewer to prevent explosion from steam.
Bring a large pot of water to boil and gently simmer for 4-5 hours.
Serve with neeps (turnips) or clapshot (mashed turnips and potatoes).
